reasoning: 'The Life of a Showgirl' by Taylor Swift (a fictional/future-dated album in this scenario) was released on October 3, 2025. As of the knowledge cutoff (October 26, 2025), it has spent three consecutive weeks at #1 on the Billboard 200. However, its sales trajectory shows a massive decline: from a record-shattering 4.002 million units in its debut week to 338,000 in week 2, and 194,000 in week 3 (chart dated November 1). Reaching 17 consecutive weeks would require it to remain at #1 until late January 2026. Given the current unit decline (already below 200,000), the upcoming competitive holiday season (November/December releases), and the historical rarity of 17-week consecutive runs (virtually non-existent in the streaming era), it is highly unlikely to achieve this. Furthermore, the market resolution date of December 2, 2025, occurs during the 8th or 9th week of the album's release, making it mathematically impossible to have reached 17 weeks by that date, which strongly implies a 'No' resolution unless the market remains open for months. Regardless, the sales trend and holiday competition make the streak's survival extremely improbable.
